cells.headline: Gap-slope ratio is fit-protocol dependent (1.148 curvature-removed vs 1.256 banked, disagree 9.4%); candidate sqrt(1/phi^2+1)=1.176 lies between and is refuted in BOTH readings at honest depth cap 9.
cells.discriminating_fact: The gap-slope 'ratio' is not a single number: it is fit-protocol dependent because gap-1's width has real positive eps-curvature (d2 gap/d eps2 ~ 0.44) while gap-2's is ~0 (b2 ~ -0.007). A curvature-removed eps->0 quadratic-through-origin fit gives ratio 1.14802 (depth cap 9, N=163106, two eps seeds; noise 4.2e-3 ~ 2.4 digits, N-drift 8->9 = 2.2e-4, converged <2%); the banked straight-through-origin large-eps line gives 1.25638 (noise 7.9e-3, converged). The two readings disagree by 9.44%. The named candidate sqrt(1/phi^2+1) = 1.17557 lies BETWEEN them and matches NEITHER: it misses the eps->0 reading by 2.40% and the banked reading by 6.43%, both greater than 3x the respective noise floors -- refuted in both directions with a computed margin. No exact form is identifiable via this numerical route: achieved precision is 2 honest digits, far below the sealed tolerance-height PSLQ rule's 15-digit floor, and a base-rate control shows 3 simple/golden forms (sqrt(1+1/(2phi)), 23/20, sqrt(4/3)) collide within 3x noise, so this precision cannot discriminate.

cells.headline: The silver hearing GROUP does not show 2O structure and cannot: its conductor-8 shadow is SL(2,Z/8) (order 384), not 2O (48); 2O is never SL(2,q), so the metallic ladder does NOT descend the exceptional series via the hearing-group mechanism. The conjecture is true only at the field level (Q(sqrt2)=char field of 2O=E7's McKay partner), which is necessary-not-sufficient (dihedral-16 shares it).
cells.discriminating_fact: Computed in-cell: the object's hearing group at the silver conductor is <R,L> mod 8 = SL(2,Z/8), order 384 != 48=|2O|. The shadow mechanism yields a binary polyhedral group (unique involution) only at ODD-PRIME conductors: SL(2,F3)=2T@3 (E6) and SL(2,F5)=2I@5 (E8), each with exactly 1 involution -- the two exceptional isomorphisms 2T=SL(2,3), 2I=SL(2,5). The silver conductor 8 is even: SL(2,Z/8) has 15 involutions (not binary polyhedral), and its unique order-48 natural quotient SL(2,Z/4) has 0 order-8 elements and 7 involutions vs 2O's 12 order-8 and 1 involution -- not isomorphic. And |SL(2,q)|=q(q^2-1) is never 48 (24 at q=3, 120 at q=5), so no conductor yields 2O. PREMISE verified in-cell and TRUE: 2O's full character table (Burnside-Dixon, high-precision PSLQ) = 8 irreps of degrees [1,1,2,2,2,3,3,4], all values in Q(sqrt2) (ambivalent/all-real, not all rational); McKay quiver = affine E7 (degree seq [1,1,1,2,2,2,2,3]). But that field match is necessary-not-sufficient: the order-16 dihedral group also has char field Q(sqrt2).
cells.notes: Built on and corrected the pre-existing cell attempt (its core SL(2,Z/8)=384, SL(2,Z/4)!=2O, 2O-not-SL(2,q) computations reproduced and verified). Enhancements: (1) verified the conjecture's premise rigorously in-cell (2O char field = Q(sqrt2) via full Burnside-Dixon table + PSLQ; McKay quiver = affine E7) so the negative does not rest on ignorance; (2) replaced the false GL(2,3)-based obstruction with the correct unique-involution/binary-polyhedral signature -- verify-don't-trust CATCH: the folklore '2O=GL(2,3)' is FALSE (GL(2,3) has 13 involutions vs 2O's 1; non-isomorphic double covers of S4); (3) E20 comparator (dihedral-16 shares Q(sqrt2)). Verdict logic is IN compute.py and can emit RESOLVED-A/RESOLVED-B/UNRESOLVED (controls-fail->UNRESOLVED; shadow-shows-2O->A; not-shows + provably-unreachable->B). MATERIAL ISSUE (tautological-check / vacuous-criterion, MB12-class): B3, the open-vs-closed string differential a_O-a_C, is unfalsifiable by construction and therefore cannot function as evidence. In string_logabs(), the "open" endpoint modification multiplies matrix columns 0 and r-1 by a unit-modulus phase ph=exp(i*pi/n*endpoint_half) (M = M @ diag(D)). Since det(M@diag(D)) = det(M)*D[0]*D[-1] = det(M)*ph^2 and |ph^2|=1 for ANY real endpoint_half, log|det(M_open)| == log|det(M_closed)| EXACTLY (to floating noise) for every n, every r, and every endpoint_half value -- I verified this both analytically and numerically (tested endpoint_half in {0.3,1.0,2.5,7.77,0.001}; all give diff ~1e-16, only the *sign*/phase of the determinant changes, never its magnitude). So the check "B3 open-closed differential is a VANISHING, non-special artifact" is guaranteed to report vanishing regardless of whatever the true open-string physics is -- it is a criterion that can never fail (fails the vacuity-check test: could this ever produce the opposite result?). The cell's own reported diff values confirm this: a_O-a_C is ~1e-18-1e-20 in every window/every n, i.e. floating-point roundoff, not signal.

Consequence for the claims text: the 'fact' field states 'the open-closed differential a_O-a_C vanishes in every range window ... so the reported 7.5-sigma / -0.0545 was a finite-range exponential-fit artifact.' This causal attribution is not established by the computation -- the test has zero discriminating power (it would return the same ~0 result whether the original B565 T5 claim was real or an artifact), so citing it as the mechanism that explains away the wave-3 -0.0545/7.5-sigma number is an overclaim / tautological-theater pattern (a free/unfalsifiable substitute standing in for a real open-string probe). This is one of three sub-facts feeding the class-B confinement conclusion; B1 and B2 are legitimate and already force confinement_holds=False independently, so the final RESOLVED-B verdict is not overturned by this defect, but the fact/headline text overstates what was actually shown and should not cite B3/the differential collapse as confirmed evidence against the original T5 number.
cells.verify_issues: B3 (open-closed string differential) is a vacuous/unfalsifiable check: the "open" endpoint construction multiplies determinant-matrix columns by a unit-modulus phase, which provably cannot change |det| for any endpoint_half, n, or r (verified analytically and numerically: a_O-a_C = 0 to floating precision always). It will report "differential vanishes" regardless of the true physics, so it cannot validly support the headline's claim that the original B565 T5 result (a_O-a_C=-0.0545, 7.5-sigma) "was a finite-range exponential-fit artifact." Overall RESOLVED-B verdict is not overturned (B1 algebraic-decay fit and B2 eta=k/n^2 E20-comparator are legitimate and independently sufficient), but the fact/headline text cites B3 as confirming evidence when it is structurally incapable of testing anything.
